verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url=https://developers.squarespace.com/, but this is simply the official Squarespace developer documentation site, NOT a dedicated MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. No evidence of an MCP server is provided.
The URL points to Squarespace's own developer documentation, not an MCP server implementation.

Evidence confirms OAuth2 is required for Extensions and third-party apps. The first evidence URL is unavailable but the OAuth page confirms the OAuth2 flow, and references to API keys are mentioned in the record.
Evidence from websitebuilderexpert.com shows paid plans (Basic $14.40, Core $20.70, Plus $35.10, Advanced) are required. No free tier is evident.

The record states Commerce API access requires Commerce Advanced paid subscription and mentions 14-day trial but needs paid plan. The pricing evidence shows only paid plans exist, supporting this claim.
Evidence shows REST API endpoints (e.g., GET https://login.squarespace.com/api/1/login/oauth/provider/authorize). Website API and Commerce APIs are described as REST-style resources.
Evidence shows Website API, Products, Orders, Inventory APIs with read-only and read-write capabilities. 'Medium' breadth seems reasonable for commerce-focused APIs.
Website API page explicitly states 'read-only access' to Website, Member, and StorePage resources, confirming the record's note.
Marked as 'buildable-with-caveats' due to paid plan requirement. This is appropriate given that Commerce Advanced is needed for full API access.
The paid Commerce Advanced plan requirement is supported by the evidence. The mention of unofficial API scraping in some third-party implementations is flagged but not directly contradicted.
The URL https://commerce-apis.squarespace.com/commerce-apis/authentication-and-permissions is marked as unavailable, so cannot be verified.
The one-liner accurately summarizes the API capabilities (products, orders, inventory, website/store data), auth methods (API keys, OAuth 2.0), and the paid plan requirement based on the evidence.

revised.name: Squarespace
revised.one_liner: Squarespace Commerce APIs let developers manage products, orders, inventory and website/store data using API keys or OAuth 2.0, but access requires a paid Commerce Advanced plan.

auth_methods.notes: Generated in site developer settings; used as Bearer token for custom applications.
Required for Squarespace Extensions and third-party apps; also required for webhook subscriptions which don't support API keys.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier; Commerce API access generally requires the Commerce Advanced paid subscription. A 14-day free trial exists but full commerce API usage needs a paid plan.

api_surface.notes: Covers Commerce APIs: Website, Products, Orders, Inventory, Webhooks, etc. Read-only in some areas (e.g., Website API).

revised.main_blocker: Requires a paid Commerce Advanced plan for full API access; some third-party MCP implementations rely on unofficial/internal API scraping rather than official endpoints.

evidence.supports: Describes API key and OAuth2 Bearer token authentication methods.
Details Website API resources and OAuth-authenticated member access.
OAuth2 flow required for Extensions and webhook subscriptions.
No free tier; paid plans required for commerce features.

flags: No free tier - paid Commerce Advanced plan required for full API access
Some existing MCP servers reportedly use unofficial/internal API scraping instead of official credentials
Webhook subscriptions require OAuth2 only, not API keys
